title: NOC 70012 – Facility operation and maintenance managers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Facility Operation and Maintenance Managers (NOC 70012) are the strategic leaders ensuring the efficiency, safety, and sustainability of Canada’s physical infrastructure. As the country pushes toward "Net-Zero" targets for the 2030s, these managers are no longer just maintaining buildings; they are implementing Smart Building technologies and managing complex energy-retrofit projects. Operating at the TEER 0 (Management) level, they oversee massive budgets and multi-disciplinary teams across diverse sectors, including aviation, healthcare, and high-tech warehousing.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category0 (Management occupations)Major Group70 – Middle management occupations in trades and transportationSub-Major Group700 – Middle management occupations in trades and transportation

duties: Strategic Operations: Planning and evaluating the operations of commercial, transportation, and recreational facilities and real estate.
Budgetary Control: Planning and managing facility operations/maintenance budgets and overseeing the leasing of space.
Project Oversight: Directing construction projects to modify or upgrade facilities and overseeing the repair of mechanical and electrical systems.
Service Management: Directing administrative services such as security, cleaning, parking, safety inspections, and snow removal.
Compliance & Safety: Developing schedules for safety inspections and preventive maintenance programs to meet 2026 environmental standards.
employment_requirements: Education: A university degree or college diploma in Facility Management, Engineering, or Business Administration is typically required.
Experience: Several years of supervisory experience in facility operations or maintenance are mandatory.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in Property Management Software, data analysis, and blueprint reading.
Specialized Licensing: Certain roles (like Cemetery Managers in Ontario) require specific provincial licensing (e.g., Bereavement Authority of Ontario).
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is rated as Good to Very Good due to a surge in infrastructure investment and a high volume of senior-level retirements:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. As of March 2026, Ontario is actively recruiting through targeted OINP draws, specifically in Southwestern, Northern, and Eastern Ontario to support regional economic development.

Alberta: Strong demand. Managers in Alberta earn some of the highest wages in the country, with an average salary of $91,755/year and a significant shortage of skilled personnel in the industrial and construction sectors.
Yukon & Atlantic Canada: Good prospects. These regions are prioritizing facility managers to oversee public administration, culture, and tourism infrastructure projects.
National Trend: Over 90% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time, with roughly 16% choosing self-employment as independent facility consultants.
Why Work in Canada as a Facility Manager?
Technological Leadership: Lead the integration of AI-driven preventive maintenance, IoT-connected HVAC systems, and digital twin building models.
High-Tier Compensation: As a management professional, your median hourly wage in Canada is $45.20/hr, with high-earners in provinces like Alberta reaching upwards of $91.72/hr.
Cross-Industry Versatility: Your skills are in demand in real estate (31% of the workforce), transportation/warehousing (11%), and public administration.
Immigration Priority: In 2026, this NOC has been a consistent target for Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) invitations, offering a fast-track route to Permanent Residency.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): In March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in this NOC under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. Scores for the Southwestern region draw started at 53.

Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): As a TEER 0 management role, it is eligible for all primary federal draws. A provincial nomination can add 600 points to your score, guaranteeing an invitation.
BC PNP Construction/Tech Streams: Highly technical maintenance managers (e.g., data center or specialized plant managers) are often targeted for BC's priority processing.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: A reliable route for managers already working in Alberta’s industrial or commercial facility sectors.
